chunks.title: Cross-Page Semantic Mapping-Centric Invoice Information Extraction for Long Documents with Complex Layout Using FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2
chunks.text:
## European Modern Studies Journal

available at www.journal-ems.com

DOI: 10.59573/emsj.9(5).2025.92

## Cross-Page Semantic Mapping-Centric Invoice Information Extraction for Long Documents with Complex Layout Using FSL-HALayoutLMv2

Ranadheer Reddy Charabuddi Sr. Lead SAP Opentext VIM Consultant, Avventis Inc, Northlake, Texas, USA ORCID: 0009-0000-4842-6148

Abstract. Automatically extracting keywords and structured information from long invoices is needed for large organizations that receive thousands of invoices. But, the conventional studies didn't capture the cross -page semantic continuity in lengthy and complex documents, leading to poor accuracy. Thus, this paper presents Few Shot Learning-based Hierarchical Attention Layout-aware Language Model version 2 (FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2)based cross-page semantic continuity capturing-aware invoice information extraction for long documents. Primarily, the invoice document image is gathered; afterward, they are preprocessed. The texts are extracted by using Open Text Optical Character Recognition (Open Text OCR) from the pre-processed invoice images. Further, the extracted text is pre-processed, followed by keyword extraction based on Named Entity Recognition (NER). Similarly, by employing Cosine Similarity-based Graph Autoencoders (CS-GAEs), cross-page semantic mapping is performed for the extracted text. Likewise, the table structure is recognized from the pre-processed invoice image based on Custom Scoring Hough line Transform-based Bottleneck Attention TableNet (CSHT-BA-TNet). Afterward, features are extracted from the recognized table structure and cross-page semantic mapping. Now, visual features are extracted from the Open Text OCR outcomes, followed by clustering based on Density-Based Spatial Keane Polar Text Clustering of Application with Noise (DBSK-PTCAN). The invoice information is extracted by using FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2 regarding the extracted keywords, clustering outcomes, and extracted features. Here, FSL is included to learn the unseen patterns. As per the results, the proposed model achieved a high accuracy of 98.61%.

Keywords : Cross-page semantic mapping, Few Shot Learning-based Hierarchical Attention Layout-aware Language Model version 2 (FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2), Invoice information extraction, Custom Scoring Hough line Transform -based Bottleneck Attention TableNet (CSHT-BA-TNet), Table structure recognition, Open Text Optical Character Recognition (Open Text OCR), and Long documents with complex layout

## Introduction

Usually, invoices are important documents for organizations as they serve as proof of purchase; also, invoices are required for accounting and tax purposes (Saout et al., 2024). Key information, including purchase date, description of goods or services, price, and payment terms are encompassed in Invoices (Kim et al., 2022). Similarly, for reducing the risk of errors, digitizing invoices is significant (Jung et al., 2021). Yet, extracting key information from multipage invoices that consist of tables, nested fields, and diverse formatting remains a challenge (Limam et al., 2023). Lately, to extract the key information from invoices, many Artificial Intelligence (AI) techniques have been introduced (Baviskar et al., 2021).

chunks.title: Cross-Page Semantic Mapping-Centric Invoice Information Extraction for Long Documents with Complex Layout Using FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2
chunks.text:
Keywords : Cross-page semantic mapping, Few Shot Learning-based Hierarchical Attention Layout-aware Language Model version 2 (FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2), Invoice information extraction, Custom Scoring Hough line Transform -based Bottleneck Attention TableNet (CSHT-BA-TNet), Table structure recognition, Open Text Optical Character Recognition (Open Text OCR), and Long documents with complex layout

OCR and EasyOCR were used to extract the text from invoice images in existing studies (Kumar & Revathy, 2021) (Boas et al., 2025). Likewise, for recognizing table structures, some existing works employed TableNet (Akdoğan & Kurt, 2024). Similarly, for extr acting the information from long invoice documents, Artificial Neural Network (ANN), NER, and transformer techniques were utilized (Lima et al., 2021) (Salgado & Sánchez, 2023). Yet, the existing studies didn't capture cross -page semantic continuity in lengthy documents. The motivation of the proposed model is to capture the relationship between the cross-pages in large documents. This improves the accuracy of invoice information extraction. Therefore, FSL-HALayoutLMv2-based cross-page semantic continuity capturing-aware invoice information extraction is proposed.

## Problem Statement

1. None of the existing works focused on capturing cross-page semantic continuity in long, complex documents, thus affecting accurate information extraction.
2. Due to the diverse content structures, the existing (Yu et al., 2020) struggled to extract the structured information.
3. The conventional (Stanisławek et al., 2021) ignored the visual cues, thus missing/incorrectly labelling the significant key details.
4. When the entities appeared in different positions across invoices, the prevailing work failed to identify the accurate key fields.
5. The prevailing works didn't learn about non -uniform layouts.

## Objectives

- → CS-GAEs are introduced to perform cross-page semantic mapping for accurate invoice information extraction.
- → CSHT-BA-TNet is employed to extract the structured information from lengthy documents.
- → Visual features are extracted; afterward, DBSK-PTCAN is established to group similar characteristics.
- → NER is used to identify the accurate key fields.
- → Few-Shot Learning (FSL) is included to learn the non-uniform layouts.

This paper is structured as: the literature survey is illustrated in Section 2, the proposed research methodology is conveyed in Section 3, the result is exemplified in Section 4, and finally, Section 5 concludes the proposed model with future scope.

## Literature Survey

(Yu et al., 2020) offered a model for key information extraction from documents. Here, to extract the key information, the improved graph learning-convolutional network was employed. The model had a rich semantic representation. However, owing to the diverse content structures, the model struggled to extract the structural information.

(Stanisławek et al., 2021) presented a key information extraction framework for lengthy documents. Here, the Layout-Aware Multi-modal BERT (LAMBERT) was used for effective key information extraction. The model was suitable for complex information extraction tasks. Nevertheless, it ignored the visual cues, thus missing/incorrectly labelling the significant key details.

chunks.title: Cross-Page Semantic Mapping-Centric Invoice Information Extraction for Long Documents with Complex Layout Using FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2
chunks.text:
(Stanisławek et al., 2021) presented a key information extraction framework for lengthy documents. Here, the Layout-Aware Multi-modal BERT (LAMBERT) was used for effective key information extraction. The model was suitable for complex information extraction tasks. Nevertheless, it ignored the visual cues, thus missing/incorrectly labelling the significant key details.

(Ma et al., 2022) discovered a robust table structure detection model. The Faster Regionbased CNN and spatial CNN were utilized for recognizing the table structures from images. The model excellently recognized the tables with complex structures. Yet, it had poor generalization to non-uniform layouts.

(Zhang et al., 2021) recommended a recognition system for financial ticket images. Here, to recognize the ticket images, the EfficientNet-b7 technique was used. The model attained improved efficiency. But, it might give error outcomes due to poor pre-processing.

(Vincent & Hari, 2022) offered a model for invoice information extraction. Here, to extract the invoice information, the CNN algorithm with a regularization technique was introduced. The model attained high performance metrics regarding F1-score. Nevertheless, it failed to distinguish characters in challenging fonts.

## Proposed Cross-Page Semantic Mapping-Centric Invoice Information Extraction for Long Documents with Complex Layout Methodology

Here, to extract the information from the invoices, the proposed FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2 is employed. In Figure 1, the proposed model's pictorial layout is shown.

Figure 1. Pictorial layout of the proposed model

<!-- image -->

## Invoice Document Image

Initially, the 'kleister -charity' dataset is gathered to train the proposed model, which contains invoice document images with significant key information and is denoted as i K  .

## Pre-Processing

Then, to improve the invoice image quality, i K  is pre-processed.

## 1. Denoising

Primarily, by sing the Median Filter (MF), the unwanted noise or distribution is eliminated from i K  u while preserving the edges and textures. MF is robust against outliers and less sensitive to extreme pixel values. The noise-removed image ( ) ( ) m,n T  is given as,

Here, median signifies the median value and ( ) gus a,b exemplifies the Gaussian function with pixel coordinates ( ) a,b .

## 2. Contrast Enhancement

Afterward, by using Contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE), the contrast enhancement is done for ( ) ( ) m,n T  . It excellently handles the images with uneven lighting conditions.

Firstly, the ( ) ( ) m,n T  is divided into a v number of non-overlapping rectangular tiles ( ) c J .

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

Here, v J signifies the th v tiles. Afterward, based on the number of pixels, the number of greyscales ( ) Gs , and ( ) 1 12 -= ,...,Gs , Hg , the histogram ( ) Hg is estimated for each c J .

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

Where, ( ) x  specifies the number of pixels. Then, the histogram is clipped above a threshold clip limit ( ) th .

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

Here,  Hg indicates the clipped histogram. Next, the conditional probability function ( ) u Cd is computed for the clipped histogram. Afterward, the ( ) u Cd is mapped into a new output intensity value. Next, for interpolating the pixel values, the binary interpolation is employed. The contrast-enhanced images are indicated as enh  .

The contrast-enhanced images are indicated as enh  .

## 3. Binarization

Afterward, binarization is performed on the enh  , which converts the enh  into a binary image; here, each pixel is assigned either 0 or 1. Thus, the pre-processed invoice image (binarized image) is specified as i G .

## Text Extraction

Next, based on Open Text OCR, the text is extracted from the i G . Generally, Open Text OCR employs open-source tools and libraries for extracting and converting the printed text from images and scanned documents into editable text .

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

Where, t  states the extracted text.

## Pre-Processing

Afterward, to improve the keyword extraction, the t  are pre-processed. Initially, the t  are converted into small tokens and are specified as  . Afterward, the misspelled words are detected and replaced with the most probable correct alternative words. The spelling-corrected words ( ) w mS are expressed as,

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

Later, the stop words (i.e., 'is', 'for', 'of', and 'and') are eliminated from the ( ) w mS . Eventually, the pre-processed text (stop word removed text) is implied as j P .

## Keyword Extraction

The keywords, including invoice number, date, total amount, vendor name, etc., are extracted using NER from j P . Usually, the NER technique identifies and classifies the key entities in text into predefined categories. The extracted keywords are indicated as   .

## Cross-Page Semantic Mapping

Afterwards, the relationship between the cross pages is analysed from t  using CS-GAEs for excellent invoice information extraction in multi-page invoice documents. Graph Autoencoders (GAEs) employ neighborhood information to learn context-aware representations of entities across pages. Nevertheless, graph-based models often rely on spatial distance between nodes. Thus, semantic-aware edge weights are employed in GAEs. Step 1: Primarily, the input graph ( ) Y is defined as,

Here, L indicates the latent node embeddings,  represents the adjacency matrix of the graph,   specifies the normalized adjacency matrix, R represents the rectified linear unit function, and  s illustrates semantic-aware edge weights, which help to provide meaningful relationships more accurately.

Step 3: The decoder reconstructs the adjacency matrix by employing L .

## Table Structure Recognition

Similarly, from i G , the table structure is recognized for making the invoice information extraction easier. TableNet (TNet) is designed with fewer parameters and has low computational complexity. Still, it struggles with documents that have unusual orientations, handwriting, stamps, or overlays. Therefore, Bottleneck Attention Module is included in TableNet. Likewise, TableNet lacks the capability to identify the internal structure of tables. Therefore, Hough Line Transform (HLT) is used. However, the Hough Line Transform randomly samples subsets of edge points. Therefore, the Custom Scoring Function (CSF) is employed.

Primarily, for extracting the feature map, the shared encoder (learns a hierarchical representation of the table region) is employed.

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

Where, H signifies the extracted feature map, enc implies the encoder operation, R ~ implies the real number, g and q represent the height and width of i G , respectively. Next, the Bottleneck Attention Module is included for handling unusual orientations, handwriting, stamps, or overlays.

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

Here, att Ch and att Sp signify the channel and spatial attention map, correspondingly, and  is the attention-refined feature map. Afterward, the table region decoder ( ) tb Dec assesses the binary mask for table regions.

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

Thus, the recognized table structure is exemplified as tb  .

Thus, the recognized table structure is exemplified as tb  .

## Feature Extraction

Features, such as nodes, edges, node position, node feature matrix, adjacency matrix, visual degree, etc., are extracted from y  . Likewise, the features, including table region, rows and columns, cell coordinates, cell merging, grid layout, etc., are extracted from the tb  . The extracted features are signified as   x .

## Visual Feature Extraction

Similarly, the visual features like font size, font weight, alignment, position, etc., are extracted from t  and are denoted as s V .

## Clustering

The visually similar regions (i.e., titles, headers, table rows, etc) are clustered regarding s V by employing DBSK-PTCAN for accurate invoice information extraction. Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (DBSCAN) makes the clustering precise. Yet, DBSCAN is sensitive to input data while choosing the epsilon value. Hence, the epsilon value is estimated using the Polar Text (PT) distance. Also, the nearby points within the epsilon value are expanded employing the Keane function.

Initially, the epsilon parameter (  ) , which assesses the neighborhood radius for each data point, is computed based on PT.

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

Here, ( ) 1 1  , M and ( ) 2 2  , M specifies the polar coordinates of the individual feature points ( ) 2 1 V ,V , M and  denotes the radial distance from the center and angle, respectively, and sf exemplifies a scaling factor. Afterward, for forming the clusters, minpts ( ) is used, which is estimated using the Keane function.

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

Afterward, the core point ( ) Cr that helps in detecting the clusters is computed.

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

The visually similar regions are clustered regarding (  ) , ( ) , and ( ) Cr . The clustered patterns are indicated as r Cl .

## Invoice Information Extraction

The invoice information is extracted based on r Cl ,   x , and   by employing FSL-HALayoutLMv2. Generally, Layout-Aware Language Model version 2 (LayoutLMv2) effectively understands complex documents. Nevertheless, it struggles to process large documents, leading to information loss. Likewise, FSL is employed to learn the uncommon patterns. Thus, in LayoutLMv2, Hierarchical Attention is employed.

Figure 2. Diagrammatic representation of the proposed FSL-HA- LayoutLMv2

<!-- image -->

Here, the inputs r Cl ,   x , and   are considered as h A . For each token in h A , the visual/text token embeddings ( ) p Z are computed as,

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

Here, tx p Z , seg p Z , ps d p Z -1 , ps d p Z -2 , and vis p Z specify the text embedding, segment identifier, 1-dimensional position embedding, 2-dimensional position embedding, and visual embedding, respectively. Afterward, the ( ) p Z are fed into the transformer encoder. Here, Hierarchical Attention ( ) A  is employed to process the large documents.

## Result and Discussion

Here, the comparative evaluation of the proposed and existing works is done, and the proposed model is implemented in the working platform of PYTHON.

## Dataset Description

The proposed model employs the 'kleister -charity' dataset, which contains invoice document images. This dataset is gathered from publicly available sources, and the dataset link is provided in the reference section. This dataset contains 2,788 annual financial reports of charity organizations, 61,643 unique pages, and 21,612 entities to extract. Among that, 80% is employed for training and 20% is wielded for testing. In Table 1, the sample image results are depicted.

Table 1. Sample image results

<!-- image -->

## European Modern Studies Journal

<!-- image -->

Table 1 displays the image results for processes, such as denoising, contrast enhancement, and binarization.

## Performance Analysis

Here, the performance analysis is done for the proposed and conventional techniques.

Table 2. Comparative validation for cross-page semantic mapping

| Methods | Graph Generation Time (ms) | Reconstruction Loss | Binary Cross- Entropy |
|------------------|------------------------------|-----------------------|-------------------------|
| Proposed CS-GAEs | 4125 | 0.083 | 0.4652 |
| GAEs | 5241 | 0.612 | 1.3629 |
| GNN | 5974 | 1.263 | 4.9562 |
| GAN | 6246 | 4.963 | 7.4556 |
| GIN | 6793 | 7.341 | 9.6714 |

chunks.title: Cross-Page Semantic Mapping-Centric Invoice Information Extraction for Long Documents with Complex Layout Using FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2
chunks.text:
Table 2. Comparative validation for cross-page semantic mapping

The comparative validation of the proposed and prevailing techniques is depicted in Table 2. Here, the proposed CS-GAEs obtained less graph generation time (4125ms), reconstruction loss (0.083), and binary cross-entropy (0.4652) due to the usage of semantic-aware edge weights . Nevertheless, the prevailing GAEs, Graph Neural Network (GNN), Graph Attention Network (GAN), and Graph Isomorphism Network (GIN) attained poor performance.

Figure 3. Graphical representation based on performance metrics

<!-- image -->

Table 3. Performance evaluation for invoice information extraction

| Methods | Precision (%) | Recall (%) | F-Measure (%) | Accuracy (%) | Sensitivity (%) | Specificity (%) |
|-----------------------------|-----------------|--------------|-----------------|----------------|-------------------|-------------------|
| Proposed FSL-HA- LayoutLMv2 | 98.15 | 98.59 | 99.82 | 98.61 | 98.59 | 98.12 |
| LayoutLMv2 | 95.46 | 95.76 | 96.34 | 95.82 | 95.76 | 95.15 |
| TILT | 92.31 | 94.74 | 94.61 | 93.59 | 94.74 | 90.83 |
| Donut | 90.76 | 92.31 | 92.36 | 91.53 | 92.31 | 88.43 |
| DocFormer | 86.24 | 87.28 | 88.61 | 87.43 | 87.28 | 85.85 |

The graphical representation of the proposed FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2 and existing methods are depicted in Figure 3 and Table 3. Here, the proposed FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2 achieved high accuracy (98.61%), precision (98.15%), recall (98.59%), F-Measure (99.82%), sensitivity (98.59%), and specificity (98.12%). Nevertheless, limited performance is attained by the conventional LayoutLMv2, Text-Image-Layout Transformer (TILT), Document Understanding Transform (Donut), and Document Transformer (DocFormer).

Figure 4. Pictorial analysis regarding (a) AUC graph and (b) ROC graph

<!-- image -->

In Figure 4, Pictorial analysis regarding the Area Under the Curve (AUC) and Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) graph are depicted. Here, the AUC graph measures the overall effectiveness of the model in discriminating between positive and negative classes. Likewise, the ROC graph evaluates the performance of a binary classification model . The proposed FSLHA-LayoutLMv2 attained better performance than the existing techniques from the analysis of AUC and ROC.

Figure 5. Computational complexity validation

<!-- image -->

The computational complexity validation of the proposed FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2 and existing methods regarding big O notation is depicted in Figure 5. Here, the proposed FSL-HALayoutLMv2 attained a low computational complexity of O(1), whereas the existing LayoutLMv2, TILT, Donut, and DocFormer had high computational complexities.

Table 4. Comparative assessment for table structure recognition

| Techniques | IoU | Precision (%) | Recall (%) |
|-----------------------|-------|-----------------|--------------|
| Proposed CSHT-BA-TNet | 0.984 | 98.23 | 98.36 |
| TNet | 0.942 | 96.56 | 96.69 |
| CTN | 0.901 | 94.37 | 94.82 |
| TT | 0.882 | 91.72 | 91.93 |
| PLN | 0.863 | 89.36 | 89.61 |

Figure 6. Performance evaluation regarding (a) IoU and (b) Precision and Recall

<!-- image -->

In Figure 6 and Table 4, the performance evaluation of the proposed CSHT-BA-TNet and conventional techniques is shown. Here, the proposed CSHT-BA-TNet achieved high Intersection over Union (IoU) (0.984), precision (98.23%), and recall (98.36%). However, the conventional TNet, Cascade TableNet (CTN), Table Transformer (TT), and PubLayNet (PLN) attained poor performance.

chunks.title: Cross-Page Semantic Mapping-Centric Invoice Information Extraction for Long Documents with Complex Layout Using FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2
chunks.text:
In Figure 6 and Table 4, the performance evaluation of the proposed CSHT-BA-TNet and conventional techniques is shown. Here, the proposed CSHT-BA-TNet achieved high Intersection over Union (IoU) (0.984), precision (98.23%), and recall (98.36%). However, the conventional TNet, Cascade TableNet (CTN), Table Transformer (TT), and PubLayNet (PLN) attained poor performance.

Figure 7. Graphical analysis regarding (a) Clustering time and (b) Dice score

<!-- image -->

The graphical analysis of the proposed DBSK-PTCAN and existing techniques are depicted in Figure 7. Here, the proposed DBSK-PTCAN attained a low clustering time (11236ms) and a high Dice score (0.962). Nevertheless, the prevailing DBSCAN, K-Means, Fuzzy C-Means (FCM), and Clustering Large Applications (CLARA) obtained a high average clustering time (20450ms) and low average dice score (0.88).

## Comparative Assessment

Here, the comparative assessment is done for the proposed model and related works.

Table 5. Comparative assessment

| Authors' name | Techniques | Advantages | Limitations |
|-----------------------|--------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| Proposed Model | FSL-HA- LayoutLMv2 | The proposed model improved the performance by capturing cross-page semantic continuity. | Yet, it didn't integrate the DevOps methodologies. |
| (Lin et al., 2021) | BERTgrid with CNN | It achieved high performance metrics. | It had less robustness for style variations. |
| (Zhang et al., 2022) | MGAN | The model had high effectiveness. | It had high computational complexity. |
| (Hamri et al., 2024) | GNN | It attained greater performance even with fewer parameters. | The model ignored the spatial relationships. |
| (Dutta et al., 2024) | VTLNLT | The research achieved high accuracy. | It had lack of explicit modeling for layout structure. |
| (Biswas et al., 2021) | ResNeXt-101- CNN | It obtained superior performance for complex layouts. | It failed to integrate textual semantics. |

The comparative assessment of the proposed FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2 and related works are depicted in Table 5. Here, by capturing cross-page semantic continuity, the proposed FSLHA-LayoutLMv2 improved the performance. Nevertheless, poor performance is attained by the existing Bidirectional Encoder Representation from Transformers grid with Convolutional Neural Network (BERTgrid with CNN), Multi-modal Graph Attention Network (MGAN), Graph Neural Network (GNN), Vision-based Transfer Learning and Natural Language-based Transformers (VTLNLT), and Residual Network-101 base CNN (ResNeXt-101-CNN).

## Conclusion

Here, this paper presented FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2-based cross-page semantic continuity capturing-aware invoice information extraction for lengthy documents. Here, essential processes, including table structure recognition, cross-page semantic mapping, keyword extraction, clustering, and invoice information extraction, were performed. For cross-page semantic mapping, the proposed CS-GAEs took a minimum graph generation time of 4125ms. Similarly, the proposed FSL-HA-LayoutLMv2 achieved high accuracy and precision of 98.61% and 98.15%, respectively for extracting the information from invoices. Thus, the proposed framework attained high trustworthiness.

## Future Enhancement

Thus, advanced techniques will be developed in the future to enhance invoice processing automation via the integration of DevOps methodologies.
